steps.title: Identify the confirming signal
steps.content: The passage contrasts Toffler's specific failed predictions with his fundamental claim that rapid change causes disorientation and atomization. The key signal: we're explicitly in "our age of disequilibrium and fragmentation," which confirms his prediction.

steps.title: Apply foresight to the choices
steps.content:
The blank describes Toffler's fundamental claim in light of "our age of disequilibrium and fragmentation"—meaning the prediction came true.

A. Prescient means having foresight; knowing what would happen before it did. Toffler predicted disorientation and atomization; we now live in an age of disequilibrium and fragmentation. The prediction was accurate, so "remarkably prescient" fits. Correct.

B. Articulate means clearly expressed. Whether Toffler stated his claim clearly says nothing about whether it turned out to be right. Eliminated.

C. Iconoclastic means attacking cherished beliefs or conventions. The passage is evaluating whether the prediction proved true, not whether it was unconventional. Eliminated.

D. Equivocal means ambiguous or open to two interpretations. The passage presents Toffler's claim as validated, not as unclear. Eliminated.

A is correct.
